dimensions.name: Question Quality
dimensions.pass_criteria: Every question represents a genuine challenge a skeptical customer would raise — about risk, cost, workflow change, data, competition, or failure modes. A new PM could not have predicted all of them easily.
dimensions.fail_criteria: Questions are softballs ('How do I get started?', 'What are the benefits?') or leading questions that assume a positive answer. The set feels like a FAQ written by the marketing team, not a skeptical customer.

dimensions.name: Answer Completeness
dimensions.pass_criteria: Every question has either a substantive answer or is explicitly marked [OPEN — owner: X] with a named owner. No question is left unanswered without acknowledgment.
dimensions.fail_criteria: One or more questions have no answer and are not marked [OPEN]. Or questions are marked [OPEN] without a named owner (e.g. just 'TBD' or blank).

dimensions.name: No Evasive Answers
dimensions.pass_criteria: Each answer directly resolves the question asked. Answers that reveal genuine uncertainty are marked [OPEN] rather than dressed up as answers.
dimensions.fail_criteria: One or more answers restate the question without resolving it, deflect to vague future plans ('We will address this later'), or use marketing language to avoid a hard truth.

dimensions.name: Coverage of Critical Concerns
dimensions.pass_criteria: The FAQ covers the customer concerns most likely to block adoption: data/privacy, workflow change, cost/value, failure modes, and differentiation from alternatives. A customer finishing this FAQ should have their most important concerns addressed.
dimensions.fail_criteria: The FAQ is missing one or more critical concern categories that a reasonable customer in the target segment would raise. The omission feels intentional rather than accidental.
